problem text: Consider this matrix $ \left[ \begin{array}{cc} -5 & 2 \\ -7 & 4 \end{array} \right] $. It has $2$ eigenvalues, $a$ and $b$. Determine these eigenvalues and express the answer as $a^2 + b^2$.
problem solution: To find the characteristic equation, solve for $$\lambda$$ such that $$\det(A - \lambda I) = 0$$. We calculate $$A - \lambda I = \begin{bmatrix} -5 - \lambda & 2 \\ -7 & 4 - \lambda \end{bmatrix}$$. This results in the characteristic equation $$(-5-\lambda)(4-\lambda) + 14 = \lambda^2 + \lambda - 6 = 0$$. Factoring yields $$(\lambda + 3)(\lambda - 2) = 0$$, with solutions $$\lambda = -3$$, $$\lambda = 2$$. Substituting these values, we find $$(-3)^2 + 2^2 = 13$$.
problem hint: How is the characteristic equation determined?
